mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and eases financial conditions, which supports higher equity prices over the next month. The signal is confirmed when the policy rate is declining from a prior elevated level rather than merely flat.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are negative in most periods where FEDFUNDS is falling for at least 2 consecutive monthly observations, or if rolling 3-year rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A renewed rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening household income and slower demand, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism is active when unemployment is deteriorating, not when it is already high but stable.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the signal has no negative edge during downturns in a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A broad retail sales acceleration in RSAFS indicates stronger consumer spending and near-term revenue support for cyclicals, lifting equities over the following week. The effect should be visible when sales growth is improving rather than merely level.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS increases for 2 consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if retail-sales-up episodes do not produce positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the average 5-day return is not greater than the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO together with rising PAYEMS signals a synchronized real-activity recovery, which should improve earnings expectations and support equities over the next quarter. The signal fails if industrial output improves without labor-market confirmation.
failure_condition: Reject if joint rises in INDPRO and PAYEMS are not followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if either series alone explains the effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is rising while FEDFUNDS remains pinned near zero, real rates decline and liquidity conditions stay supportive, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect depends on inflation pressure exceeding policy tightening.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds FEDFUNDS by at least 2 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-above-policy episodes do not outperform over 20 days, or if positive SPX returns are absent when CPIAUCSL accelerates and FEDFUNDS stays below 1%.

mechanism_narrative: A weak housing-starts level in HOUST signals fading construction demand and broader cyclical softness, which should weigh on equities over the next quarter. The signal is stronger when housing activity is already below prior-cycle norms.
candidate_trigger: HOUST is below its 6-month moving average by more than 5%
failure_condition: Reject if low-HOUST episodes are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the drawdown relationship disappears after controlling for the broader market trend.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in GDPC1 and RSAFS signals that both aggregate output and final demand are recovering, which should translate into stronger equity performance over the next quarter. The mechanism fails if one series improves while the other contracts.
failure_condition: Reject if joint upturns in GDPC1 and RSAFS fail to predict positive 60-day SPX returns, or if either variable alone produces the same effect without the interaction.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-unemployment regime, a decline in UNRATE from an elevated level indicates labor-market healing and should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is specifically a recovery signal and should not operate when unemployment is low and flat.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE remains above 8.5% and then falls for 2 consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if high-unemployment declines do not lead to positive 20-day SPX returns more often than baseline, or if PAYEMS does not improve alongside falling UNRATE in successful cases.
